name: Letta (MemGPT lineage)
context_primitives: Letta continues the MemGPT idea: structured memory compartments—message buffer, editable core memory blocks, recall memory over full history, and archival memory backed by external stores. Agents can update memory via tools; background consolidation ('dreaming') is described in product docs.
deployment_pattern: CLI, desktop app, Python/TypeScript SDKs; self-host or cloud per Letta deployment options in docs.
benchmarks_eval_alignment: Academic MemGPT lineage; modern product claims should be checked against Letta release notes and blog benchmarks [uncertain].
ecosystem_integrations: Vector/graph archival backends; integrates with agent tool loops; docs for Letta Code memory APIs.
differentiators: First-class **agent-editable** memory blocks vs. passive log scraping; emphasizes persistent agent identity.
significance_for_builders: Use when the product needs **explicit memory editing** and explainability (what the agent believes about the user) more than opaque embedding retrieval.
key_timeline_or_milestones: Oct 2025 Letta blog on rearchitecting agent loop (MemGPT → tool-centric v1 lessons).
